topic: Wheres your legs daddy long legs
title: Where Are Your Legs, Daddy Long Legs? – Children (Ages 9–11)

hunks.text: Daddy long legs are not spiders, even though they look a little like spiders. They are their own special creature called HARVESTMEN. Harvestmen have eight very long, thin LEGS that make them easy to spot. These legs are so skinny and delicate that they look like threads. Their BODIES are very small and round, almost like a tiny marble with legs attached. You can find daddy long legs in gardens, forests, and sometimes in your HOUSE.

questions.question: What is the real name for a daddy long legs?
How many legs does a daddy long legs have?
Name something the text tells you about a daddy long legs' body.
questions.answer: VERY SMALL / ROUND / LIKE A TINY MARBLE

questions.question: Besides gardens and forests, where else might you find creatures that look like bugs?

hunks.imageAlt: macro photography of orange and gray insect on green petaled flower

hunks.text: Those long legs are perfect for what daddy long legs need to do every day. Their legs help them WALK across grass, through leaves, and over rocks. The legs are very FLEXIBLE, which means they can bend and move in many different directions. Even though the legs look weak, they are actually strong enough to carry the daddy long legs' body. Each leg has tiny CLAWS at the end that help them grip surfaces and climb. Without those long legs, daddy long legs could not move around to find food or stay safe.

questions.question: What do daddy long legs use their legs to do?
What word describes how daddy long legs' legs can bend in different directions?
What can daddy long legs do because they have long legs?
questions.answer: MOVE AROUND / FIND FOOD / STAY SAFE

questions.question: Stand up and stretch your arms out as wide as you can, like daddy long legs stretching their legs. What part of your body stretches the longest?

hunks.imageAlt: woman in black skirt and black leather shoes

hunks.text: Daddy long legs are HUNTERS. They hunt for tiny bugs and other small creatures to eat. Scientists believe they also munch on dead PLANTS, mold, and sometimes even their own waste, which sounds gross but helps them survive in nature. Unlike spiders, daddy long legs do not make webs to catch their food. Instead, they hunt by walking around and looking for meals. Their eyesight is not very good, so they use their long legs like feelers to sense what is around them.

questions.question: What word describes what daddy long legs are because they hunt for food?
Do daddy long legs make webs like spiders do?
Name something the text says daddy long legs eat.
questions.answer: TINY BUGS / SMALL CREATURES / DEAD PLANTS / MOLD

questions.question: If a daddy long legs eats three tiny bugs in the morning and five tiny bugs in the afternoon, how many bugs did it eat in total?

hunks.imageAlt: macro photography of orange and gray insect on green petaled flower

hunks.text: One of the coolest things about daddy long legs is that they can drop their legs when they feel scared or trapped. This is called AUTOTOMY, which is a word that means 'self-loss.' When a predator, like a bird or a bigger spider, grabs one of their legs, the daddy long legs can separate that leg from its body and escape. The dropped leg wiggles and moves around on the ground, which distracts the predator. This trick gives the daddy long legs time to run away and SURVIVE.

questions.question: What is the word for when daddy long legs drops a leg to escape?
What does the dropped leg do to help the daddy long legs?
Name something that might grab a daddy long legs' leg.
questions.answer: A BIRD / A BIGGER SPIDER / A PREDATOR

questions.question: If you had to drop something to stay safe, what would you want to happen to help you escape?

hunks.text: Daddy long legs live in lots of different PLACES around the world. They like cool, dark, and damp areas where they do not get too hot or dry. You will find them hiding under rocks, in BUSHES, under logs, and in leaf PILES. Some daddy long legs live in caves, and some live in people's BASEMENTS. They are most ACTIVE at night, which means they hunt and move around when it is dark. During the day, they stay hidden in shady spots to rest.

questions.question: What kind of places do daddy long legs like to live in?
When are daddy long legs most active?
Name a place where you might find daddy long legs.
questions.answer: UNDER ROCKS / IN BUSHES / UNDER LOGS / IN LEAF PILES / IN CAVES / IN BASEMENTS

questions.question: What other creatures do you know about that are also most active at night?

hunks.text: Here is something surprising: daddy long legs have been around for a very long time. FOSSILS show that creatures similar to today's daddy long legs lived millions of years ago. Scientists study these ancient fossils to learn how daddy long legs have CHANGED or stayed the same over time. Daddy long legs are one of the oldest types of ARACHNIDS—the same group that includes spiders and scorpions. Even though they are in the same group, daddy long legs are very different from spiders in many important ways.

questions.question: What do scientists find and study to learn about creatures from millions of years ago?
What large group of creatures includes daddy long legs, spiders, and scorpions?
Name something the text tells you about daddy long legs and time.
questions.answer: THEY HAVE BEEN AROUND FOR A LONG TIME / SIMILAR CREATURES LIVED MILLIONS OF YEARS AGO / SCIENTISTS STUDY HOW THEY CHANGED

questions.question: Pretend you are a scientist digging in the ground and you find a fossil of a daddy long legs from millions of years ago. What are you thinking or feeling as you discover it?

hunks.imageAlt: A picture of a skeleton sitting on a rock

hunks.text: Many people are afraid of daddy long legs because they think the creatures are dangerous or POISONOUS. This is not true at all. Daddy long legs cannot hurt humans because they do not have FANGS big enough to bite through our skin. They are not poisonous, and they do not carry diseases. In fact, daddy long legs are actually HELPFUL creatures. They eat small pests like flies and other bugs that bother us. Having daddy long legs around is like having tiny helpers that keep the bug population under control.

questions.question: Are daddy long legs poisonous?
What body part do daddy long legs lack that would let them bite through human skin?
Name a reason why daddy long legs are helpful.
questions.answer: THEY EAT SMALL PESTS / THEY EAT FLIES / THEY KEEP BUG POPULATION UNDER CONTROL

questions.question: If daddy long legs eat two flies on Monday, three flies on Tuesday, and four flies on Wednesday, how many flies did they eat in total?

hunks.imageAlt: two hands reaching up towards each other

hunks.text: The next time you see a daddy long legs, remember all the amazing things about them. They are CREATURES worth protecting, even though they look strange to us. Their long legs are not for being creepy—those legs help them survive, hunt, and escape from danger. Daddy long legs have lived on Earth for millions of years, long before humans even existed. By learning about daddy long legs, you can help other people understand that not every creature that looks weird is scary. When you see a daddy long legs, you can appreciate it for being one of nature's most interesting DESIGNS.

questions.question: What word describes daddy long legs as special living things?
How long have daddy long legs lived on Earth?
Name something the text tells you about why daddy long legs' long legs are important.
questions.answer: HELP THEM SURVIVE / HELP THEM HUNT / HELP THEM ESCAPE FROM DANGER

questions.question: What is one way you could help other people feel less scared of creatures like daddy long legs?

hunks.imageAlt: A stick insect camouflaged on a branch.
